steps.title: Place however with the first clause
steps.content: The sentence says the neoclassical writers were not the first to adopt classical modes. “However” belongs at the end of that first independent clause to contrast it with the previous sentence, and a semicolon then separates the next independent clause.

steps.title: Separate the full clauses
steps.content: Choice A and Choice B use only commas between independent clauses, creating faulty boundaries. Choice D puts “however” with the second clause, but that clause supports the first claim rather than contrasting with it. The option with “however” before the semicolon has the right logic and boundary. The answer is C.
